'''Grisserno''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] who served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Attaining the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[General/Legends|general]], he flew [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing bombers]] and proved that they were an effective [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] when used correctly despite their limitations against newer, more modern, [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. Grisserno [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] the Alliance victory at the [[Battle of Denab]], routing the [[Imperial Fourth Attack Squadron]] and was hailed as a hero for his efforts. His son, [[Welinarro]], briefly served the Alliance before leaving to work for [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] criminal underground.

==Biography==
The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]]<ref name="Sisar" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]] Grisserno was a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="SWSB" /> serving in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Starfighter Corps]].<ref name="EGTW" /> He sired a son, [[Welinarro]], who also fought for the Alliance as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], but who left the Rebellion for a life in the criminal underworld, leading a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[Gauntlet starfighter]]s based at [[Nwarcol Point]] on the [[Sisar Run/Legends|Sisar Run]].<ref name="Sisar" /> Grisserno attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[General/Legends|general]], and was noted for his skill at flying and utilizing [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]] in combat. Along with that protégé, [[Horton Salm/Legends|Horton Salm]], Grisserno proved that Y-wings could still be effective combatants against modern [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. He showed that the fighters were not required to engage in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfights]] to be effective; rather, simply accelerating towards a target and relying on the [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] and wingmen to keep the fighter's tail clean while delivering their payloads produced the desired results.<ref name="EGTW" />

During the Galactic Civil War, an assault group<ref name="SWSB" /> of Y-wings<ref name="EGTW" /> under Grisserno's command [[Battle of Denab|engaged]] the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Fourth Attack Squadron|Fourth Attack Squadron]] at [[Denab/Legends|Denab]]<ref name="SWSB" /> in the [[Sluis sector/Legends|Sluis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The ships of the Fourth Attack Squadron were mostly [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] which suffered from a slow [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight drive]] speed. At Denab, the ships of the Imperial force were split into two wings stationed too far apart to support each other. Despite the fact that the Fourth Attack Squadron had more combined firepower that his forces, Grisserno ordered his forces to attack, targeting each wing in turn, and defeating them. Due to their low sublight speed, the wings could not move fast enough to support each other, causing them to be routed by the Alliance force. Popular opinion decreed that had the Fourth Attack Squadron been comprised of [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] with faster engines, then the Battle of Denab would never have occurred as the two wings would have been able to combine their forces and deter or overwhelm their attackers.<ref name="SWSB" /> For his role in the victory, Grisserno was regarded as the "hero of the Battle of Denab."<ref name="Sisar" />

==Personality and traits==
A competent commander, Grisserno had a grasp of tactics and was able to employ them effectively in combat.<ref name="SWSB" /> He also helped to teach other Alliance members, such as Horton Salm, who would go to be one of the Alliance's top Y-wing pilots and commanders.<ref name="EGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Grisserno was first mentioned in ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'', published by [[West End Games]] in [[1987]]. When the [[The Star Wars Sourcebook (Second Edition)|second edition]] of the sourcebook was released in [[1994]], the reference to Grisserno remained. He was later mentioned in [[1997]]'s ''[[Secrets of the Sisar Run]]'', where his species was revealed, as well as his son. In [[2012]], he received a mention in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''. While it was not specifically stated that Grisserno was a Y-wing pilot, a later mention of the Battle of Denab in the same book reveals that a Y-wing group was responsible for the victory at Denab.
